strategies.name: Logical-fold nested-select metadata sanitation
strategies.target: Mutate existing `select-and-or.ll` logical fold tests (e.g. `and_or1`, `or_and1`, commuted/vector variants) to add `!prof` and `!prof` on function entry, then check both generated selects use `!{!"unknown", !"instcombine"}` (or no stale branch_weights).
strategies.rationale: The patch updates multiple logical bool folds (`(~a|c)`, `(c&b)`, `(a|c)`, `(c&~b)`, and factorization forms) to use unknown profile metadata. These rewrites create inner+outer selects; a partial conversion or missed branch can leave stale branch weights on one select.
strategies.expected_issue: After folding, one of the newly created selects still carries the old `!prof !{!"branch_weights",...}` (or only outer/inner is sanitized), causing profile inconsistency and potential profcheck verification failure.

strategies.name: SPF metadata propagation under OR/AND decomposition
strategies.target: Mutate `test_or_umax`, `test_or_umin`, `test_and_umax`, `test_and_umin` to include strong asymmetric branch weights and run profile verification checks; compare whether propagated weights should instead be unknown.
strategies.rationale: In `visitSelectInst` SPF path, new outer select is created with `MDFrom=&SI`. This assumes metadata is safe to preserve. But in patterns like `select (A||cmp), X, Y -> select A, X, umax/umin(...)`, condition used by new outer select differs from original short-circuit condition.
strategies.expected_issue: SPF canonicalization path may copy metadata from original select in cases where outer condition is structurally different from original conditional probability, leading to mis-modeled profile metadata and possible profcheck mismatch.

strategies.name: Instruction-kind sensitive unknown marking
strategies.target: Mutate logical fold tests to force/avoid constant folding (e.g., with `freeze`, extra `xor`, and multi-use blockers) and verify that no stale `branch_weights` survive and no malformed metadata appears on replaced instructions.
strategies.rationale: For some folds (`CreateLogicalOr/And` paths), unknown metadata is applied only if result `dyn_cast<Instruction>` succeeds. Further simplification/constant-folding can alter where metadata should end up.
strategies.expected_issue: Unknown-profile marking may be skipped when value folds to a non-instruction or when only one of several created instructions receives unknown metadata, silently dropping expected profile intent.

strategies.name: Profiled vs unprofiled function boundary
strategies.target: Create paired mutations of the same fold patterns: one with `!prof function_entry_count` and one without. Check profiled case gets unknown/safe metadata, unprofiled case gets no new profile metadata.
strategies.rationale: `setExplicitlyUnknownBranchWeightsIfProfiled` only sets unknown metadata when function entry count exists. Rewrites should not inject unknown in unprofiled functions, but must sanitize in profiled ones.
strategies.expected_issue: Behavior divergence between profiled and unprofiled functions: metadata may be unexpectedly injected or unexpectedly dropped, violating pass policy and causing unstable test outcomes.

strategies.name: Vector and scalable-vector metadata parity
strategies.target: Mutate existing vector tests (`and_or*_vec`, `or_and*_vec`, plus scalable forms by cloning style from other tests) with `!prof` metadata and verify transformed selects consistently carry unknown (or preserved only in SPF-designated cases).
strategies.rationale: Patch touches generic bool-select folding that handles scalar and vector i1. Existing coverage is stronger for scalar; vector/scalable logical forms are prone to path divergence in matchers and builder helpers.
strategies.expected_issue: Vector/scalable-i1 logical rewrites may miss metadata sanitation in one lane-form variant (fixed/scalable, commuted operand order), leaving stale branch weights.

reason_thou:
1. Fix Understanding
- The patch changes InstCombine select folding metadata behavior in two places:
  - SPF canonicalization path in `visitSelectInst`: when forming a new select around canonicalized SPF value (e.g., umax/umin/smax/etc), it now passes `MDFrom=&SI` to preserve profile metadata.
  - Logical bool folds in `foldSelectOfBools`: instead of copying old profile metadata into newly created selects/logical forms, it now marks new branch metadata as explicitly unknown (when profiling is enabled), via `CreateSelectWithUnknownProfile`, `createSelectInstWithUnknownProfile`, and `setExplicitlyUnknownBranchWeightsIfProfiled`.
- The intent is to avoid profile verification failures from imprecise propagation when condition structure changes, while preserving metadata in cases deemed semantics-preserving for condition evaluation.

2. Formal Semantic Modeling
- Before fix (relevant paths):
  - Some logical folds reused old select metadata (`MDFrom` from inner/original select), potentially assigning old branch weights to restructured conditions.
  - SPF canonicalization path shown in patch did not explicitly preserve metadata in this location.
- After fix:
  - Logical fold-generated selects are marked `!prof !{!"unknown", !"instcombine"}` (in profiled functions), or left without metadata in unprofiled functions.
  - SPF canonicalization now preserves metadata from original select (`MDFrom=&SI`).
- Potential semantic divergence point:
  - Branch weight metadata semantically models condition true/false likelihood. If transformed select condition differs (even if final value semantics are equivalent), directly copying weights can be imprecise.
- Concrete instantiation:
  - Original: `%r = select (A || B), X, Y` with `P(A)=0.1`, `P(B|!A)=0.9` => `P(A||B)=0.91`.
  - Transformed SPF-like outer form: `select A, X, V(B,X,Y)`.
  - New outer condition true rate is `P(A)=0.1`, not `0.91`. Copying old branch weights to this outer select encodes a different probability model.

3. Assumptions and Preconditions Identified
- Assumption A: In SPF canonicalization site, copying `SI` profile to new select is valid because condition/branch semantics are considered preserved enough.
- Assumption B: In logical fold rewrites, any inherited branch weights are too imprecise, so unknown metadata is the safest correct choice.
- Assumption C: Unknown metadata must only be injected for profiled functions (entry count > 0), otherwise avoid introducing profile metadata.
- Assumption D: All newly created select instructions in affected logical transformations are covered by unknown-marking APIs.

4. Potential Cases to Break Assumptions
- A may break when transformed outer select condition differs materially from original condition probability (short-circuit decomposition forms).
- B may break if one rewrite path still preserves old metadata on one of nested selects while marking another unknown.
- C may break in boundary cases where only branch_weights exist or profile state is unusual (paired profiled/unprofiled variants).
- D may break when builder simplification changes returned value kind (instruction vs constant) or when commuted/vector/scalable matcher path uses a different creation helper.

5. Test Strategies
- Provided above as five targeted strategies, focusing on:
  1) logical nested-fold sanitation,
  2) SPF propagation precision under decomposition,
  3) instruction-kind/folding sensitivity,
  4) profiled vs unprofiled boundary behavior,
  5) vector/scalable parity coverage.

{"found": false, "tool": "difftest", "action": "test", "original_ir": "
define <2 x i1> @test(<2 x i1> %a, <2 x i1> %b, <2 x i1> %c) !prof !0 {
  %notb = xor <2 x i1> %b, <i1 true, i1 true>
  %cond = and <2 x i1> %notb, %c
  %r = select <2 x i1> %cond, <2 x i1> %a, <2 x i1> %b, !prof !1
  ret <2 x i1> %r
}
!0 = !{!"function_entry_count", i64 1000}
!1 = !{!"branch_weights", i32 2, i32 3}

define <2 x i1> @main(i32 %argc, ptr %argv) {
entry:
  %r = call <2 x i1> @test(<2 x i1> <i1 true, i1 false>, <2 x i1> <i1 false, i1 true>, <2 x i1> <i1 true, i1 true>)
  ret <2 x i1> %r
}
", "transformed_ir": "
; ModuleID = '/data/tmp/tmpeo43d3m9/orig.ll'
source_filename = "/data/tmp/tmpeo43d3m9/orig.ll"

define <2 x i1> @test(<2 x i1> %a, <2 x i1> %b, <2 x i1> %c) !prof !0 {
  %1 = select <2 x i1> %c, <2 x i1> %a, <2 x i1> zeroinitializer, !prof !1
  %r = select <2 x i1> %b, <2 x i1> splat (i1 true), <2 x i1> %1, !prof !1
  ret <2 x i1> %r
}

!0 = !{!"function_entry_count", i64 1000}
!1 = !{!"unknown", !"instcombine"}

define <2 x i1> @main(i32 %argc, ptr %argv) {
entry:

  ret <2 x i1> %r
}

define <2 x i1> @test(<2 x i1> %a, <2 x i1> %b, <2 x i1> %c) !prof !0 {
  %not = xor <2 x i1> %c, <i1 true, i1 true>
  %and1 = and <2 x i1> %c, %a
  %and2 = select <2 x i1> %b, <2 x i1> %not, <2 x i1> zeroinitializer, !prof !1
  %or1 = or <2 x i1> %and1, %and2
  ret <2 x i1> %or1
}
!0 = !{!"function_entry_count", i64 1000}
!1 = !{!"branch_weights", i32 2, i32 3}

define <2 x i1> @main(i32 %argc, ptr %argv) {
entry:
  %r = call <2 x i1> @test(<2 x i1> <i1 true, i1 true>, <2 x i1> <i1 false, i1 true>, <2 x i1> <i1 true, i1 false>)
  ret <2 x i1> %r
}

report:
{
  "test": [
    "```llvm
define i8 @test(i8 %x, i8 %y, i1 %cond) !prof !0 {
  %cmp = icmp ugt i8 %x, %y
  %or = select i1 %cond, i1 true, i1 %cmp
  %ret = select i1 %or, i8 %x, i8 %y, !prof !1
  ret i8 %ret
}
!0 = !{!"function_entry_count", i64 1000}
!1 = !{!"branch_weights", i32 1000, i32 1}
```",
    "opt -S -passes=instcombine"
  ],
  "thoughts": "Bug Triggering Analysis:
- No miscompile was found across all required tests and mutation strategies.
- This representative SPF case shows expected behavior: InstCombine rewrites to `select %cond, %x, umax(%x,%y)` and preserves branch weights on the resulting outer select. Differential execution matched.

Fix Weakness Analysis:
- We stress-tested all 11 managed tests, and each covered all 5 Phase-1 strategies via targeted mutations:
  1) logical nested-select sanitation,
  2) SPF propagation under decomposition,
  3) instruction-kind/folding sensitivity (`freeze`, no-fold variants),
  4) profiled vs unprofiled boundary,
  5) vector parity checks.
- Observations indicate metadata handling aligns with patch intent:
  - logical restructuring in profiled functions commonly results in `!{!"unknown", !"instcombine"}`;
  - unprofiled paired variants did not gain synthetic unknown metadata;
  - SPF canonicalization preserved weights as intended.
- A few vector constructions hit tool/infra limits (llubi vector crashes and one no-fixpoint verifier issue requiring `instcombine<no-verify-fixpoint>`), but no semantic divergence between original and transformed IR was observed in completed differential checks.",
  "force": false
}
